articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the 'National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047', outlining a comprehensive strategy to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• The roadmap targets an annual production capacity of 5 million metric tonnes (MMT) by 2030, with further expansion plans by 2047, aiming to significantly reduce reliance on fossil fuels.
• Key pillars include incentivizing domestic manufacturing of electrolysers, developing dedicated green hydrogen corridors, and promoting R&D for cost reduction and efficiency.
• The policy emphasizes job creation, energy security, and achieving India's net-zero emissions target by 2070, attracting substantial private sector investment.
• It also includes provisions for pilot projects in hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and long-haul transportation, along with export promotion strategies.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Energy
Infrastructure
Investment models
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047 targets 5 MMT production by 2030.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
Focuses on domestic electrolyser manufacturing and R&D.
India's net-zero target is 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's energy security challenges and how green hydrogen can address them.
Analyze the economic potential and job creation aspects of the green hydrogen economy.
Evaluate the technological and infrastructural hurdles in achieving the targets of the roadmap.
Examine the role of international collaborations and private investment in India's green hydrogen journey.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Resource Allocation Dispute
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment today, reaffirming the principles of cooperative federalism in a dispute concerning the allocation of natural resources between two states.
• The ruling clarified the extent of state legislative power versus central government's role in matters related to inter-state rivers and mineral resources.
• The court emphasized the need for consultation and mutual agreement, urging states to resolve disputes through dialogue rather than litigation, setting a precedent for future federal conflicts.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for ongoing resource-sharing disputes and future policy formulations related to shared natural assets.
• It reiterated that while the Centre has overarching regulatory powers, the states retain substantial autonomy over resources within their territorial limits, guided by constitutional provisions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court reaffirmed principles of cooperative federalism.
Ruling on inter-state resource allocation.
Emphasized dialogue for dispute resolution.
Impacts state legislative power vs. central government's role in resource management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of this judgment on the existing inter-state river water disputes in India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in strengthening cooperative federalism in India, particularly in resource management.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in balancing the powers of the Union and the States.
Suggest institutional reforms to improve inter-state resource allocation mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: Article 263 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of an Inter-State Council to inquire into and advise upon disputes between states.

articles.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Critical Technologies and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded today, with both nations committing to deepen cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, including AI, quantum computing, and semiconductors.
• Discussions also focused on enhancing bilateral trade, aiming to reach a target of $500 billion in goods and services by 2030, addressing tariff and non-tariff barriers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, with specific initiatives discussed for maritime security and regional stability.
• The dialogue also touched upon collaboration in defence, space exploration, and climate change, highlighting the comprehensive nature of the strategic partnership.
• The two countries agreed on joint research and development projects and talent exchange programs in high-tech sectors, strengthening their innovation ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-US Strategic Dialogue held.
Focus on critical technologies (AI, quantum, semiconductors) and trade.
Target of $500 billion bilateral trade by 2030.
Commitment to free and open Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-US strategic partnership in the evolving geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging critical technology cooperation with the US.
Evaluate the impact of enhanced India-US trade relations on India's economic growth and global supply chains.
Examine how India's role in the Indo-Pacific aligns with US objectives and regional stability.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United States displayed together.
articles.trivia: The US is one of India's largest trading partners, with bilateral trade reaching over $120 billion in recent years.

articles.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged Amidst Inflationary Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, maintaining its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• The decision was largely in line with market expectations, as the central bank continues to prioritize bringing inflation within its target band of 4% (+/- 2%).
• RBI Governor highlighted persistent concerns over food inflation and global commodity price volatility, despite some moderation in core inflation.
• The MPC also revised India's GDP growth projection for FY25 slightly upwards, citing robust domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Experts suggest the RBI is adopting a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth, with potential rate cuts anticipated later if inflation consistently moderates.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Food processing and related industries in India
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Inflation target is 4% (+/- 2%).
Concerns over food inflation and global commodity prices.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools like the repo rate in managing India's inflation dynamics, particularly food inflation.
Evaluate the impact of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance on different sectors of the Indian economy.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members: three officials of the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, with a primary objective of bringing lunar samples back to Earth.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, this mission aims to demonstrate advanced capabilities in precise lunar landing, sample collection, and return trajectory.
• The mission involves a complex architecture comprising an orbiter, lander, ascent vehicle, and re-entry module, showcasing India's growing prowess in deep space exploration.
• Scientists anticipate that the returned samples will provide invaluable data for understanding the Moon's geological evolution, resource potential, and the early solar system.
• ISRO highlighted collaborations with international partners for data sharing and ground support, underscoring the global nature of space exploration endeavors.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO preparing for Chandrayaan-4 mission.
Primary objective: lunar sample return.
Mission architecture includes orbiter, lander, ascent vehicle, re-entry module.
Aims to study Moon's geology and early solar system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for scientific understanding of the Moon and the solar system.
Analyze the technological challenges and advancements required for a successful Chandrayaan-4 mission.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its implications for global space leadership.
Examine the potential for international collaboration in future Indian space missions.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft approaching the Moon.
articles.trivia: Only three countries have successfully returned samples from the Moon: the United States, Russia (then the Soviet Union), and China.

articles.headline: Election Commission Pushes for Greater Transparency in Political Funding
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today reiterated its commitment to electoral reforms, specifically advocating for greater transparency in political funding mechanisms.
• The ECI submitted fresh recommendations to the Law Ministry, proposing amendments to the Representation of the People Act, 1951, to make political donations more transparent.
• Key suggestions include bringing all political donations, including those below the current threshold, under scrutiny and mandating disclosure of donor identities for all contributions.
• This move comes amidst ongoing debates about the electoral bonds scheme and concerns raised by various civil society groups regarding opaque funding.
• The ECI emphasized that transparency in political funding is crucial for ensuring free and fair elections and strengthening democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Transparency & Accountability
prelimsPoints: ECI recommends greater transparency in political funding.
Proposes amendments to Representation of the People Act, 1951.
Suggests disclosing donor identities for all contributions.
Aims for free and fair elections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the electoral bonds scheme in India.
Analyze the role of the Election Commission of India in promoting transparency and accountability in electoral processes.
Evaluate the impact of opaque political funding on the integrity of democratic elections in India.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to ensure greater transparency in political funding, drawing lessons from international best practices.
articles.imageDescription: Logo of the Election Commission of India.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India.

articles.headline: India Champions Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Finance Track Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India advocated for broader adoption of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) among G20 nations during the Finance Track meeting, emphasizing its potential for inclusive growth.
• Finance Minister highlighted India's success with UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker as examples of how DPI can drive financial inclusion, efficient service delivery, and innovation.
• The discussions focused on developing common frameworks and standards for interoperable DPIs to facilitate cross-border digital payments and data exchange.
• India proposed a global repository of DPI solutions to enable developing countries to leverage proven models for their digital transformation journeys.
• The initiative aligns with India's G20 presidency outcomes, aiming to bridge the digital divide and foster sustainable development worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: India advocated DPI adoption at G20 Finance Track meeting.
Highlighted UPI, Aadhaar, DigiLocker as successful DPIs.
Proposed global repository for DPI solutions.
Aims to bridge digital divide and foster sustainable development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its significance for achieving inclusive growth in developing economies.
Analyze how India's experience with UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker can serve as a model for other G20 nations and the global South.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in establishing common frameworks for interoperable DPIs globally.
Examine the role of technology and digitalization in promoting financial inclusion and efficient governance.
articles.imageDescription: World map with digital network lines connecting countries, representing global DPI.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023, showcasing its massive adoption.

articles.headline: Government Expands Drone Subsidy Scheme for Agricultural Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced an expansion of the subsidy scheme for agricultural drones, aiming to boost modern farming practices.
• The revised scheme increases the financial assistance for farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and agricultural graduates for purchasing and operating drones.
• The move is intended to promote precision agriculture, reduce manual labor, and enhance efficiency in activities like pesticide spraying, nutrient assessment, and crop health monitoring.
• Training programs for drone pilots and entrepreneurs in rural areas will also be scaled up to ensure widespread adoption and skill development.
• This initiative is part of the government's broader vision to modernize Indian agriculture and improve farmers' income through technological interventions.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Technology missions
Economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India
Land reforms in India.
Effects of liberalization on the economy
prelimsPoints: Government expanded drone subsidy scheme for agriculture.
Increased financial assistance for farmers, FPOs, and agri-graduates.
Aims to promote precision agriculture and efficiency.
Part of modernizing Indian agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of integrating drone technology into Indian agriculture.
Analyze how government subsidies and training programs can facilitate the adoption of modern farming practices.
Evaluate the role of FPOs in leveraging technology for small and marginal farmers' welfare.
Examine the broader implications of technological interventions on farmers' income and agricultural productivity in India.
articles.imageDescription: Agricultural drone spraying pesticides over a field.
articles.trivia: India aims to become a global drone hub by 2030, with significant policy support for the sector.

articles.headline: National Institute of Oceanography Releases Report on Coastal Erosion
articles.explainer:
• The National Institute of Oceanography (NIO) released a comprehensive report today, highlighting the alarming rates of coastal erosion along significant stretches of India's coastline.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground surveys, identified several 'hotspots' highly vulnerable to erosion, particularly in states like West Bengal, Odisha, and Kerala.
• The report attributes the increasing erosion to a combination of natural factors (sea-level rise, extreme weather events) and anthropogenic activities (sand mining, port development).
• It recommends integrated coastal zone management plans, nature-based solutions like mangrove restoration, and regulated coastal development to mitigate the impact.
• The findings are crucial for policymakers to develop effective strategies for coastal protection and disaster preparedness, safeguarding livelihoods and ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including waterbodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: NIO report on coastal erosion in India.
Identifies hotspots in West Bengal, Odisha, Kerala.
Causes: natural factors (sea-level rise) and anthropogenic activities (sand mining).
Recommends integrated coastal zone management and nature-based solutions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted causes and consequences of coastal erosion in India, linking them to climate change and human activities.
Discuss the effectiveness of current coastal management strategies and propose innovative solutions for coastal protection.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of coastal erosion on fishing communities and coastal ecosystems.
Examine the role of scientific research and data in informing policy decisions for environmental conservation and disaster management.
articles.imageDescription: Image showing severely eroded coastline with damaged structures.
articles.trivia: Around one-third of India's 7,500 km coastline is experiencing varying degrees of erosion.

articles.headline: Progress Report on National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Released
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting key achievements and ongoing challenges.
• The report noted significant strides in curriculum reforms, particularly the development of the National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education and early childhood care.
• It also detailed efforts in teacher training, digital education initiatives (like SWAYAM and DIKSHA), and the establishment of multidisciplinary institutions.
• Challenges remain in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, equitable access to quality education in remote areas, and adequate funding for higher education reforms.
• The report emphasized continued focus on skill development, vocational education integration, and leveraging technology to personalize learning experiences.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry- the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education released NEP 2020 progress report.
Achievements in curriculum reforms (NCF) and teacher training.
Challenges: foundational literacy, equitable access, funding.
Focus on skill development and vocational education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Discuss the key challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of digital initiatives like SWAYAM and DIKSHA in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020.
Examine how vocational education and skill development can be effectively integrated into mainstream education as envisioned by NEP.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education reforms.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third such policy in India's history, after policies in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: India's Defence Sector Achieves New Milestones in Indigenization
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced significant progress in India's defence indigenization efforts, with several key platforms and systems now being produced domestically.
• The 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative has led to a reduction in defence imports and a boost in indigenous manufacturing, fostering a robust defence industrial base.
• New orders for advanced artillery systems, light combat aircraft components, and naval vessels were placed with Indian private and public sector companies.
• Emphasis was laid on enhancing R&D capabilities and encouraging private sector participation, including startups, in developing cutting-edge defence technologies.
• The push for indigenization not only strengthens national security but also creates employment opportunities and boosts technological self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Defence announced progress in indigenization.
Reduction in defence imports due to 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat'.
New orders for indigenous artillery, LCA components, naval vessels.
Focus on R&D and private sector participation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of defence indigenization for India's national security and geopolitical standing.
Analyze the challenges faced by India in achieving complete self-reliance in defence manufacturing.
Evaluate the impact of the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative on the growth of India's defence industrial base.
Examine the role of private sector and startups in driving innovation and indigenization in the defence sector.
articles.imageDescription: Indigenous Indian Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas in flight.
articles.trivia: India is among the top global defence importers, but is now rapidly pushing for indigenous production to reduce this dependency.

articles.headline: ICAR Launches New Initiatives for Climate-Resilient Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) today announced a series of new initiatives aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture across the country.
• These initiatives include developing drought-tolerant crop varieties, promoting water-efficient irrigation techniques, and integrating climate-smart farming practices.
• Focus will be on region-specific adaptation strategies, with pilot projects launched in vulnerable agricultural zones experiencing erratic weather patterns.
• ICAR emphasized capacity building for farmers and agricultural extension workers on climate change impacts and sustainable farming methods.
• The program seeks to ensure food security amidst increasing climate variability and reduce the vulnerability of farmers to extreme weather events.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: ICAR launched initiatives for climate-resilient agriculture.
Focus on drought-tolerant crops and water-efficient irrigation.
Region-specific adaptation strategies and pilot projects.
Capacity building for farmers on climate change impacts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and the imperative for climate-resilient farming practices.
Analyze the role of research institutions like ICAR in developing and disseminating climate-smart agricultural technologies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government policies in supporting farmers to adapt to climate change impacts.
Suggest policy interventions and technological solutions to enhance food security in the context of increasing climate variability.
articles.imageDescription: Farmer inspecting crops in a field, symbolizing climate-resilient agriculture.
articles.trivia: ICAR is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India.

articles.headline: India Reviews Indus Water Treaty with Pakistan Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
articles.explainer:
• India initiated a high-level review of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing concerns over Pakistan's non-cooperation and security considerations.
• The review aims to assess the feasibility of optimizing India's usage of waters from the Western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) as per treaty provisions.
• Discussions focused on accelerating the construction of hydro-power projects on these rivers, which have historically faced delays.
• While India reiterated its commitment to the treaty, the review signals a stronger stance on safeguarding its national interests and water security.
• The move comes amidst a broader reassessment of bilateral agreements in light of evolving geopolitical dynamics and cross-border issues.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India reviews Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan.
Aims to optimize usage of Western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
Focus on accelerating hydropower projects.
IWT was signed in 1960, brokered by World Bank.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
Discuss the implications of India's review of the IWT on India-Pakistan relations and regional water security.
Evaluate the strategic importance of hydropower projects on the Western rivers for India's energy and water needs.
Examine the role of international treaties in managing transboundary river disputes and the challenges of their implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the allocated rivers under the IWT.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, with the World Bank acting as a guarantor.

articles.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms for Urban Governance
articles.explainer:
• An expert committee constituted by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs submitted its report, recommending extensive reforms for urban governance in India.
• The report advocated for strengthening local self-governance, enhancing financial autonomy of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs), and professionalizing urban planning.
• Key recommendations include empowering Mayors, decentralizing decision-making, and fostering citizen participation in urban development.
• It also highlighted the need for robust data-driven urban planning, leveraging technology for smart city initiatives, and sustainable infrastructure development.
• The reforms aim to make Indian cities more livable, resilient, and economically vibrant, addressing challenges like rapid urbanization, infrastructure deficits, and environmental sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Expert panel recommends urban governance reforms.
Advocates for strengthening ULBs' financial autonomy.
Key recommendations: empowering Mayors, decentralizing decision-making.
Focus on data-driven urban planning and smart city initiatives.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major challenges facing urban governance in India and the need for comprehensive reforms.
Discuss the significance of the 74th Constitutional Amendment Act and the extent of its implementation in strengthening ULBs.
Evaluate the recommendations of the expert panel, particularly regarding financial autonomy and citizen participation.
Examine how effective urban governance can contribute to the success of smart city initiatives and sustainable urban development.
articles.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with well-planned infrastructure, symbolizing urban development.
articles.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) granted constitutional status to urban local bodies.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues Warning on Evolving Cybersecurity Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) today issued a comprehensive advisory, warning against evolving cybersecurity threats targeting critical infrastructure and government systems.
• The advisory highlighted the rise of sophisticated ransomware attacks, phishing campaigns, and supply chain vulnerabilities, urging organizations to bolster their digital defenses.
• CERT-In emphasized the importance of regular security audits, employee training, and adherence to best practices for data protection and incident response.
• The agency also detailed new guidelines for reporting cyber incidents and fostering greater collaboration between government agencies and private sector cybersecurity firms.
• The warning underscores the increasing importance of cybersecurity in national security and economic stability in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Basics of cyber security
prelimsPoints: CERT-In issued warning on evolving cybersecurity threats.
Highlighted ransomware, phishing, supply chain vulnerabilities.
Emphasized security audits, training, incident response.
New guidelines for reporting cyber incidents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyber attacks on India's critical infrastructure and the implications for national security.
Analyze the measures taken by the Indian government, including the role of CERT-In, to enhance cybersecurity capabilities.
Evaluate the challenges in combating sophisticated cyber crimes and ensuring data protection in a rapidly digitalizing economy.
Suggest a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, private sector, and individuals to build a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image of digital locks and network lines, symbolizing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: CERT-In is the national agency for performing various functions in the area of cyber security as per Section 70B of the Information Technology Act, 2000.

articles.headline: India Hosts Global Health Summit, Focus on Pandemic Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• India today commenced hosting the 'Global Health Summit', bringing together international leaders, health experts, and policymakers to discuss future pandemic preparedness.
• The summit focused on strengthening global health infrastructure, equitable access to vaccines and therapeutics, and improved surveillance systems for emerging pathogens.
• Health Minister highlighted India's experience during recent pandemics and its role as a 'pharmacy of the world' in vaccine production and distribution.
• Discussions also covered strategies for addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and the impact of climate change on public health.
• The summit aims to foster international cooperation and develop a unified global response framework for future health crises, ensuring health equity.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: India hosts Global Health Summit.
Focus on pandemic preparedness, equitable vaccine access.
India's role as 'pharmacy of the world' highlighted.
Discussions on NCDs and climate change impact on health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key lessons learned from recent global pandemics regarding health infrastructure and international cooperation.
Analyze India's role and responsibilities in strengthening global health security and ensuring equitable access to healthcare resources.
Evaluate the 'One Health' approach in addressing emerging infectious diseases and the links between human, animal, and environmental health.
Examine the challenges in achieving health equity globally and the role of international summits in forging consensus for action.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse group of medical professionals and world leaders at a conference.
articles.trivia: The 'One Health' concept recognizes that the health of humans, animals, and ecosystems are interconnected.

articles.headline: New Species of Amphibian Discovered in Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• The newly identified frog species, characterized by its unique vocalizations and distinct morphological features, highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region.
• This discovery underscores the critical need for continued ecological research and conservation efforts in the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Scientists believe that many more species await discovery in this region, which faces threats from habitat loss, climate change, and human encroachment.
• The finding contributes significantly to the understanding of amphibian diversity and evolution in India.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: New amphibian species discovered in Western Ghats.
Discovery by Zoological Survey of India (ZSI).
Western Ghats is a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO site.
Highlights need for conservation.
articles.imageDescription: Close-up image of the newly discovered frog species.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

articles.headline: Government Launches Scheme to Boost Sustainability in Textile Industry
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles unveiled a new scheme aimed at promoting sustainability, circularity, and eco-friendly practices within India's textile industry.
• The scheme provides financial incentives for textile units adopting technologies for water conservation, waste reduction, and renewable energy integration.
• It also encourages the use of sustainable raw materials, such as organic cotton and recycled fibers, and promotes responsible manufacturing processes.
• The initiative is designed to enhance the global competitiveness of Indian textiles while addressing environmental concerns and meeting international sustainability standards.
• Capacity building and skill development programs for workers in sustainable textile manufacturing will also be a key component.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Textiles launched scheme for sustainability.
Incentives for water conservation, waste reduction, renewable energy.
Promotes organic cotton and recycled fibers.
Aims to enhance global competitiveness and address environmental concerns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the environmental challenges faced by the textile industry and the importance of adopting sustainable practices.
Analyze how government schemes can incentivize green manufacturing and promote a circular economy in the textile sector.
Evaluate the potential impact of sustainable textile practices on India's export market and global image.
Examine the role of technology and innovation in transforming the textile industry towards greater environmental responsibility.
articles.imageDescription: Fabric samples with 'eco-friendly' tag, symbolizing sustainable textiles.
articles.trivia: The textile industry is one of the oldest and largest industries in India, contributing significantly to its economy and employment.

articles.headline: ASI Initiates Restoration Project for Ancient Temple Complex in Karnataka
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) commenced a major restoration project for a centuries-old temple complex in Karnataka, known for its intricate Hoysala architecture.
• The project focuses on structural stabilization, scientific conservation of sculptures, and documentation of the temple's historical and cultural significance.
• Advanced laser scanning and 3D mapping technologies are being employed to ensure accurate reconstruction and preservation of original features.
• The temple, dating back to the 12th century, is a prime example of South Indian architectural brilliance and a crucial site for historical research.
• This initiative aims to protect India's rich cultural heritage, promote tourism, and educate future generations about the country's past artistic achievements.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI initiated restoration of ancient temple in Karnataka.
Temple features Hoysala architecture, dates to 12th century.
Uses laser scanning and 3D mapping for conservation.
Aims to protect cultural heritage and promote tourism.
articles.imageDescription: Detailed carving from a Hoysala temple.
articles.trivia: Hoysala temples are known for their intricate carvings, stellate (star-shaped) plans, and use of soft soapstone.

articles.headline: Health Ministry Expands Telemedicine Services to Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced a significant expansion of its telemedicine services, particularly targeting remote and rural areas across India.
• The 'eSanjeevani' platform will be scaled up, enabling more patients to consult doctors remotely, reducing the need for travel and improving access to specialists.
• New telemedicine kiosks and health and wellness centers equipped with digital consultation facilities will be established in underserved regions.
• The initiative aims to bridge the healthcare accessibility gap, especially for primary healthcare services and chronic disease management.
• Training for community health workers and local medical practitioners in utilizing telemedicine tools will be intensified to ensure effective implementation.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Health Ministry expands telemedicine services to rural areas.
eSanjeevani platform to be scaled up.
New telemedicine kiosks and health and wellness centers.
Aims to bridge healthcare accessibility gap.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of telemedicine in transforming healthcare delivery in rural and remote areas of India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing and scaling up digital health initiatives like eSanjeevani across diverse geographical and socio-economic contexts.
Evaluate the role of technology in achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes.
Examine how policy support and infrastructure development can further strengthen India's digital health ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Doctor consulting a rural patient via video call on a tablet.
articles.trivia: eSanjeevani is a national telemedicine service that has served over 14 crore patients since its launch.

highlights: India launches National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047, aiming for 5 MMT production by 2030, to become a global hub.
Supreme Court upholds states' rights in resource allocation, reaffirming cooperative federalism principles.
India-US Strategic Dialogue focuses on critical technologies, trade, and Indo-Pacific cooperation, targeting $500 billion trade by 2030.
RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 6.5% amidst persistent inflationary concerns, maintains 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
ISRO accelerates preparations for Chandrayaan-4, an ambitious mission aimed at bringing lunar samples back to Earth.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047', consider the following statements:
1. It targets an annual production capacity of 5 million metric tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen by 2030.
2. The roadmap primarily focuses on the export of green hydrogen, with limited emphasis on domestic consumption.
3. It includes incentives for domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The roadmap targets an annual production capacity of 5 MMT by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect. The roadmap aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production *and export*, but it also emphasizes significant domestic consumption to reduce reliance on fossil fuels in various sectors. Statement 3 is correct. Key pillars include incentivizing domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Water Treaty (IWT):
1. The Treaty was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
2. Under the Treaty, India has exclusive control over the waters of the Western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
3. The Permanent Indus Commission is a bilateral commission established under the Treaty to implement and manage the Treaty.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Indus Water Treaty was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, with the World Bank as a guarantor. Statement 2 is incorrect. Under the Treaty, India has exclusive use of the Eastern rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej), while Pakistan has exclusive use of the Western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab), though India is permitted certain non-consumptive uses and limited irrigation, power generation, and storage on the Western rivers. Statement 3 is correct. The Permanent Indus Commission is mandated to meet regularly and resolve disputes arising from the Treaty.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is/are key objective(s) of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020?
1. Universal foundational literacy and numeracy.
2. Integration of vocational education from school level.
3. Promoting multidisciplinary education in higher education.
4. Establishing a single regulatory body for all higher education institutions.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct objectives of NEP 2020. NEP 2020 aims to achieve universal foundational literacy and numeracy, integrate vocational education from school, and promote multidisciplinary education. Statement 4 is incorrect. While NEP 2020 proposes a single overarching regulator for higher education, the Higher Education Commission of India (HECI), it envisions separate verticals for regulation, accreditation, funding, and standard-setting, not a single body that directly regulates all aspects. It's a regulatory *framework* rather than a single body for all functions.

prelimsMocks.question:
The term 'Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)' often seen in news, refers to:
(a) Government-owned physical infrastructure like roads and railways that are digitally managed.
(b) Open and interoperable digital systems and platforms that enable the delivery of public and private services.
(c) Satellite communication networks exclusively for public broadcasting services.
(d) Blockchain-based systems for secure public record-keeping only.
options: (a) Government-owned physical infrastructure like roads and railways that are digitally managed.
(b) Open and interoperable digital systems and platforms that enable the delivery of public and private services.
(c) Satellite communication networks exclusively for public broadcasting services.
(d) Blockchain-based systems for secure public record-keeping only.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Open and interoperable digital systems and platforms that enable the delivery of public and private services.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems like identity systems (Aadhaar), payment systems (UPI), and data exchange platforms (DigiLocker) that are essential for a society to function and are accessible to all. They are open, interoperable, and designed to facilitate public and private services.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is the national agency for performing various functions in the area of cyber security in India, as per the Information Technology Act, 2000?
options: (b) National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national nodal agency for responding to computer security incidents as and when they occur. It is mandated under Section 70B of the Information Technology Act, 2000, to perform various functions in the area of cyber security.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambitious National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047 presents both significant opportunities and formidable challenges for its energy transition. Elaborate. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the roadmap's goal (global hub, 5 MMT by 2030). Discuss opportunities like energy security, decarbonization, job creation, and export potential. Then, outline challenges such as high production costs, technological scaling, infrastructure development (storage, transport), water availability, and securing investments. Conclude with the need for a holistic approach.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent reaffirmation of states' rights in resource allocation underscores the complexities of cooperative federalism in India. Analyze the constitutional provisions governing inter-state resource disputes and suggest mechanisms to foster better cooperation. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States; Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure; Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by explaining cooperative federalism and the context of the SC ruling. Detail constitutional provisions like Articles 262 (inter-state river disputes) and 263 (Inter-State Council), and the Seventh Schedule. Discuss challenges like political will, lack of scientific data, and judicial overreach. Suggest mechanisms like empowered Inter-State Council, transparent data sharing, basin-level planning, and alternative dispute resolution methods.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the significance of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in achieving inclusive growth and efficient service delivery in India. How can India's experience with DPI be leveraged for global digital transformation, particularly for developing countries? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and its role in India (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker). Explain how it fosters financial inclusion, reduces corruption, and enables efficient public service delivery. For global leveraging, discuss the 'India Stack' as a model, open-source approach, cost-effectiveness for developing nations, and potential for cross-border interoperability. Address challenges like data privacy, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps.

quickBytes.text: The 'National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047' aims for 5 MMT production by 2030.
Article 262 of the Indian Constitution deals with adjudication of disputes relating to waters of inter-State rivers.
The G20 Finance Track meeting discussed broader adoption of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has six members: three from RBI and three external experts.
Chandrayaan-4 mission's primary objective is to bring lunar samples back to Earth.
The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority.
The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) granted constitutional status to urban local bodies.
CERT-In is India's national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Hoysala temples are known for their intricate carvings and stellate (star-shaped) plans.

GS1.headline: National Institute of Oceanography Releases Report on Coastal Erosion
GS1.explainer:
• The National Institute of Oceanography (NIO) released a comprehensive report today, highlighting the alarming rates of coastal erosion along significant stretches of India's coastline.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground surveys, identified several 'hotspots' highly vulnerable to erosion, particularly in states like West Bengal, Odisha, and Kerala.
• The report attributes the increasing erosion to a combination of natural factors (sea-level rise, extreme weather events) and anthropogenic activities (sand mining, port development).
• It recommends integrated coastal zone management plans, nature-based solutions like mangrove restoration, and regulated coastal development to mitigate the impact.
• The findings are crucial for policymakers to develop effective strategies for coastal protection and disaster preparedness, safeguarding livelihoods and ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including waterbodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: NIO report on coastal erosion in India.
Identifies hotspots in West Bengal, Odisha, Kerala.
Causes: natural factors (sea-level rise) and anthropogenic activities (sand mining).
Recommends integrated coastal zone management and nature-based solutions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted causes and consequences of coastal erosion in India, linking them to climate change and human activities.
Discuss the effectiveness of current coastal management strategies and propose innovative solutions for coastal protection.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of coastal erosion on fishing communities and coastal ecosystems.
Examine the role of scientific research and data in informing policy decisions for environmental conservation and disaster management.
GS1.imageDescription: Image showing severely eroded coastline with damaged structures.
GS1.trivia: Around one-third of India's 7,500 km coastline is experiencing varying degrees of erosion.

GS1.headline: ASI Initiates Restoration Project for Ancient Temple Complex in Karnataka
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) commenced a major restoration project for a centuries-old temple complex in Karnataka, known for its intricate Hoysala architecture.
• The project focuses on structural stabilization, scientific conservation of sculptures, and documentation of the temple's historical and cultural significance.
• Advanced laser scanning and 3D mapping technologies are being employed to ensure accurate reconstruction and preservation of original features.
• The temple, dating back to the 12th century, is a prime example of South Indian architectural brilliance and a crucial site for historical research.
• This initiative aims to protect India's rich cultural heritage, promote tourism, and educate future generations about the country's past artistic achievements.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI initiated restoration of ancient temple in Karnataka.
Temple features Hoysala architecture, dates to 12th century.
Uses laser scanning and 3D mapping for conservation.
Aims to protect cultural heritage and promote tourism.
GS1.imageDescription: Detailed carving from a Hoysala temple.
GS1.trivia: Hoysala temples are known for their intricate carvings, stellate (star-shaped) plans, and use of soft soapstone.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Resource Allocation Dispute
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment today, reaffirming the principles of cooperative federalism in a dispute concerning the allocation of natural resources between two states.
• The ruling clarified the extent of state legislative power versus central government's role in matters related to inter-state rivers and mineral resources.
• The court emphasized the need for consultation and mutual agreement, urging states to resolve disputes through dialogue rather than litigation, setting a precedent for future federal conflicts.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for ongoing resource-sharing disputes and future policy formulations related to shared natural assets.
• It reiterated that while the Centre has overarching regulatory powers, the states retain substantial autonomy over resources within their territorial limits, guided by constitutional provisions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court reaffirmed principles of cooperative federalism.
Ruling on inter-state resource allocation.
Emphasized dialogue for dispute resolution.
Impacts state legislative power vs. central government's role in resource management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of this judgment on the existing inter-state river water disputes in India.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in strengthening cooperative federalism in India, particularly in resource management.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in balancing the powers of the Union and the States.
Suggest institutional reforms to improve inter-state resource allocation mechanisms.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: Article 263 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of an Inter-State Council to inquire into and advise upon disputes between states.

GS2.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Critical Technologies and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded today, with both nations committing to deepen cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, including AI, quantum computing, and semiconductors.
• Discussions also focused on enhancing bilateral trade, aiming to reach a target of $500 billion in goods and services by 2030, addressing tariff and non-tariff barriers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, with specific initiatives discussed for maritime security and regional stability.
• The dialogue also touched upon collaboration in defence, space exploration, and climate change, highlighting the comprehensive nature of the strategic partnership.
• The two countries agreed on joint research and development projects and talent exchange programs in high-tech sectors, strengthening their innovation ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-US Strategic Dialogue held.
Focus on critical technologies (AI, quantum, semiconductors) and trade.
Target of $500 billion bilateral trade by 2030.
Commitment to free and open Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-US strategic partnership in the evolving geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging critical technology cooperation with the US.
Evaluate the impact of enhanced India-US trade relations on India's economic growth and global supply chains.
Examine how India's role in the Indo-Pacific aligns with US objectives and regional stability.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United States displayed together.
GS2.trivia: The US is one of India's largest trading partners, with bilateral trade reaching over $120 billion in recent years.

GS2.headline: Election Commission Pushes for Greater Transparency in Political Funding
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today reiterated its commitment to electoral reforms, specifically advocating for greater transparency in political funding mechanisms.
• The ECI submitted fresh recommendations to the Law Ministry, proposing amendments to the Representation of the People Act, 1951, to make political donations more transparent.
• Key suggestions include bringing all political donations, including those below the current threshold, under scrutiny and mandating disclosure of donor identities for all contributions.
• This move comes amidst ongoing debates about the electoral bonds scheme and concerns raised by various civil society groups regarding opaque funding.
• The ECI emphasized that transparency in political funding is crucial for ensuring free and fair elections and strengthening democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Transparency & Accountability
prelimsPoints: ECI recommends greater transparency in political funding.
Proposes amendments to Representation of the People Act, 1951.
Suggests disclosing donor identities for all contributions.
Aims for free and fair elections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the electoral bonds scheme in India.
Analyze the role of the Election Commission of India in promoting transparency and accountability in electoral processes.
Evaluate the impact of opaque political funding on the integrity of democratic elections in India.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to ensure greater transparency in political funding, drawing lessons from international best practices.
GS2.imageDescription: Logo of the Election Commission of India.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India.

GS2.headline: India Champions Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Finance Track Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India advocated for broader adoption of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) among G20 nations during the Finance Track meeting, emphasizing its potential for inclusive growth.
• Finance Minister highlighted India's success with UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker as examples of how DPI can drive financial inclusion, efficient service delivery, and innovation.
• The discussions focused on developing common frameworks and standards for interoperable DPIs to facilitate cross-border digital payments and data exchange.
• India proposed a global repository of DPI solutions to enable developing countries to leverage proven models for their digital transformation journeys.
• The initiative aligns with India's G20 presidency outcomes, aiming to bridge the digital divide and foster sustainable development worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Highlighted UPI, Aadhaar, DigiLocker as successful DPIs.
Proposed global repository for DPI solutions.
Aims to bridge digital divide and foster sustainable development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its significance for achieving inclusive growth in developing economies.
Analyze how India's experience with UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker can serve as a model for other G20 nations and the global South.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in establishing common frameworks for interoperable DPIs globally.
Examine the role of technology and digitalization in promoting financial inclusion and efficient governance.
GS2.imageDescription: World map with digital network lines connecting countries, representing global DPI.
GS2.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023, showcasing its massive adoption.

GS2.headline: Progress Report on National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Released
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting key achievements and ongoing challenges.
• The report noted significant strides in curriculum reforms, particularly the development of the National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for school education and early childhood care.
• It also detailed efforts in teacher training, digital education initiatives (like SWAYAM and DIKSHA), and the establishment of multidisciplinary institutions.
• Challenges remain in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, equitable access to quality education in remote areas, and adequate funding for higher education reforms.
• The report emphasized continued focus on skill development, vocational education integration, and leveraging technology to personalize learning experiences.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry- the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education released NEP 2020 progress report.
Achievements in curriculum reforms (NCF) and teacher training.
Challenges: foundational literacy, equitable access, funding.
Focus on skill development and vocational education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Discuss the key challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the role of digital initiatives like SWAYAM and DIKSHA in achieving the objectives of NEP 2020.
Examine how vocational education and skill development can be effectively integrated into mainstream education as envisioned by NEP.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education reforms.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third such policy in India's history, after policies in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India Reviews Indus Water Treaty with Pakistan Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
GS2.explainer:
• India initiated a high-level review of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing concerns over Pakistan's non-cooperation and security considerations.
• The review aims to assess the feasibility of optimizing India's usage of waters from the Western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) as per treaty provisions.
• Discussions focused on accelerating the construction of hydro-power projects on these rivers, which have historically faced delays.
• While India reiterated its commitment to the treaty, the review signals a stronger stance on safeguarding its national interests and water security.
• The move comes amidst a broader reassessment of bilateral agreements in light of evolving geopolitical dynamics and cross-border issues.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India reviews Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan.
Aims to optimize usage of Western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
Focus on accelerating hydropower projects.
IWT was signed in 1960, brokered by World Bank.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
Discuss the implications of India's review of the IWT on India-Pakistan relations and regional water security.
Evaluate the strategic importance of hydropower projects on the Western rivers for India's energy and water needs.
Examine the role of international treaties in managing transboundary river disputes and the challenges of their implementation.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the allocated rivers under the IWT.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, with the World Bank acting as a guarantor.

GS2.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms for Urban Governance
GS2.explainer:
• An expert committee constituted by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs submitted its report, recommending extensive reforms for urban governance in India.
• The report advocated for strengthening local self-governance, enhancing financial autonomy of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs), and professionalizing urban planning.
• Key recommendations include empowering Mayors, decentralizing decision-making, and fostering citizen participation in urban development.
• It also highlighted the need for robust data-driven urban planning, leveraging technology for smart city initiatives, and sustainable infrastructure development.
• The reforms aim to make Indian cities more livable, resilient, and economically vibrant, addressing challenges like rapid urbanization, infrastructure deficits, and environmental sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

Focus on data-driven urban planning and smart city initiatives.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major challenges facing urban governance in India and the need for comprehensive reforms.
Discuss the significance of the 74th Constitutional Amendment Act and the extent of its implementation in strengthening ULBs.
Evaluate the recommendations of the expert panel, particularly regarding financial autonomy and citizen participation.
Examine how effective urban governance can contribute to the success of smart city initiatives and sustainable urban development.
GS2.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with well-planned infrastructure, symbolizing urban development.
GS2.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) granted constitutional status to urban local bodies.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Global Health Summit, Focus on Pandemic Preparedness
GS2.explainer:
• India today commenced hosting the 'Global Health Summit', bringing together international leaders, health experts, and policymakers to discuss future pandemic preparedness.
• The summit focused on strengthening global health infrastructure, equitable access to vaccines and therapeutics, and improved surveillance systems for emerging pathogens.
• Health Minister highlighted India's experience during recent pandemics and its role as a 'pharmacy of the world' in vaccine production and distribution.
• Discussions also covered strategies for addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and the impact of climate change on public health.
• The summit aims to foster international cooperation and develop a unified global response framework for future health crises, ensuring health equity.

Analyze India's role and responsibilities in strengthening global health security and ensuring equitable access to healthcare resources.
Evaluate the 'One Health' approach in addressing emerging infectious diseases and the links between human, animal, and environmental health.
Examine the challenges in achieving health equity globally and the role of international summits in forging consensus for action.
GS2.imageDescription: Diverse group of medical professionals and world leaders at a conference.
GS2.trivia: The 'One Health' concept recognizes that the health of humans, animals, and ecosystems are interconnected.

GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced a significant expansion of its telemedicine services, particularly targeting remote and rural areas across India.
• The 'eSanjeevani' platform will be scaled up, enabling more patients to consult doctors remotely, reducing the need for travel and improving access to specialists.
• New telemedicine kiosks and health and wellness centers equipped with digital consultation facilities will be established in underserved regions.
• The initiative aims to bridge the healthcare accessibility gap, especially for primary healthcare services and chronic disease management.
• Training for community health workers and local medical practitioners in utilizing telemedicine tools will be intensified to ensure effective implementation.
