GS3.headline: India Unveils Ambitious National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the 'National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047', outlining a comprehensive strategy to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
• The roadmap targets an annual production capacity of 5 million metric tonnes (MMT) by 2030, with further expansion plans by 2047, aiming to significantly reduce reliance on fossil fuels.
• Key pillars include incentivizing domestic manufacturing of electrolysers, developing dedicated green hydrogen corridors, and promoting R&D for cost reduction and efficiency.
• The policy emphasizes job creation, energy security, and achieving India's net-zero emissions target by 2070, attracting substantial private sector investment.
• It also includes provisions for pilot projects in hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and long-haul transportation, along with export promotion strategies.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Energy
Infrastructure
Investment models
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Roadmap 2047 targets 5 MMT production by 2030.
Aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
Focuses on domestic electrolyser manufacturing and R&D.
India's net-zero target is 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's energy security challenges and how green hydrogen can address them.
Analyze the economic potential and job creation aspects of the green hydrogen economy.
Evaluate the technological and infrastructural hurdles in achieving the targets of the roadmap.
Examine the role of international collaborations and private investment in India's green hydrogen journey.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration depicting green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged Amidst Inflationary Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, maintaining its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• The decision was largely in line with market expectations, as the central bank continues to prioritize bringing inflation within its target band of 4% (+/- 2%).
• RBI Governor highlighted persistent concerns over food inflation and global commodity price volatility, despite some moderation in core inflation.
• The MPC also revised India's GDP growth projection for FY25 slightly upwards, citing robust domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Experts suggest the RBI is adopting a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth, with potential rate cuts anticipated later if inflation consistently moderates.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Food processing and related industries in India
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Inflation target is 4% (+/- 2%).
Concerns over food inflation and global commodity prices.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools like the repo rate in managing India's inflation dynamics, particularly food inflation.
Evaluate the impact of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance on different sectors of the Indian economy.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members: three officials of the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, with a primary objective of bringing lunar samples back to Earth.
• Building on the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, this mission aims to demonstrate advanced capabilities in precise lunar landing, sample collection, and return trajectory.
• The mission involves a complex architecture comprising an orbiter, lander, ascent vehicle, and re-entry module, showcasing India's growing prowess in deep space exploration.
• Scientists anticipate that the returned samples will provide invaluable data for understanding the Moon's geological evolution, resource potential, and the early solar system.
• ISRO highlighted collaborations with international partners for data sharing and ground support, underscoring the global nature of space exploration endeavors.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO preparing for Chandrayaan-4 mission.
Primary objective: lunar sample return.
Mission architecture includes orbiter, lander, ascent vehicle, re-entry module.
Aims to study Moon's geology and early solar system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of lunar sample return missions for scientific understanding of the Moon and the solar system.
Analyze the technological challenges and advancements required for a successful Chandrayaan-4 mission.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its implications for global space leadership.
Examine the potential for international collaboration in future Indian space missions.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft approaching the Moon.
GS3.trivia: Only three countries have successfully returned samples from the Moon: the United States, Russia (then the Soviet Union), and China.

GS3.headline: Government Expands Drone Subsidy Scheme for Agricultural Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced an expansion of the subsidy scheme for agricultural drones, aiming to boost modern farming practices.
• The revised scheme increases the financial assistance for farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and agricultural graduates for purchasing and operating drones.
• The move is intended to promote precision agriculture, reduce manual labor, and enhance efficiency in activities like pesticide spraying, nutrient assessment, and crop health monitoring.
• Training programs for drone pilots and entrepreneurs in rural areas will also be scaled up to ensure widespread adoption and skill development.
• This initiative is part of the government's broader vision to modernize Indian agriculture and improve farmers' income through technological interventions.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Technology missions
Economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India
Land reforms in India.
Effects of liberalization on the economy
prelimsPoints: Government expanded drone subsidy scheme for agriculture.
Increased financial assistance for farmers, FPOs, and agri-graduates.
Aims to promote precision agriculture and efficiency.
Part of modernizing Indian agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of integrating drone technology into Indian agriculture.
Analyze how government subsidies and training programs can facilitate the adoption of modern farming practices.
Evaluate the role of FPOs in leveraging technology for small and marginal farmers' welfare.
Examine the broader implications of technological interventions on farmers' income and agricultural productivity in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Agricultural drone spraying pesticides over a field.
GS3.trivia: India aims to become a global drone hub by 2030, with significant policy support for the sector.

GS3.headline: India's Defence Sector Achieves New Milestones in Indigenization
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced significant progress in India's defence indigenization efforts, with several key platforms and systems now being produced domestically.
• The 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative has led to a reduction in defence imports and a boost in indigenous manufacturing, fostering a robust defence industrial base.
• New orders for advanced artillery systems, light combat aircraft components, and naval vessels were placed with Indian private and public sector companies.
• Emphasis was laid on enhancing R&D capabilities and encouraging private sector participation, including startups, in developing cutting-edge defence technologies.
• The push for indigenization not only strengthens national security but also creates employment opportunities and boosts technological self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Defence announced progress in indigenization.
Reduction in defence imports due to 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat'.
New orders for indigenous artillery, LCA components, naval vessels.
Focus on R&D and private sector participation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of defence indigenization for India's national security and geopolitical standing.
Analyze the challenges faced by India in achieving complete self-reliance in defence manufacturing.
Evaluate the impact of the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiative on the growth of India's defence industrial base.
Examine the role of private sector and startups in driving innovation and indigenization in the defence sector.
GS3.imageDescription: Indigenous Indian Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas in flight.
GS3.trivia: India is among the top global defence importers, but is now rapidly pushing for indigenous production to reduce this dependency.

GS3.headline: ICAR Launches New Initiatives for Climate-Resilient Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) today announced a series of new initiatives aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture across the country.
• These initiatives include developing drought-tolerant crop varieties, promoting water-efficient irrigation techniques, and integrating climate-smart farming practices.
• Focus will be on region-specific adaptation strategies, with pilot projects launched in vulnerable agricultural zones experiencing erratic weather patterns.
• ICAR emphasized capacity building for farmers and agricultural extension workers on climate change impacts and sustainable farming methods.
• The program seeks to ensure food security amidst increasing climate variability and reduce the vulnerability of farmers to extreme weather events.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: ICAR launched initiatives for climate-resilient agriculture.
Focus on drought-tolerant crops and water-efficient irrigation.
Region-specific adaptation strategies and pilot projects.
Capacity building for farmers on climate change impacts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and the imperative for climate-resilient farming practices.
Analyze the role of research institutions like ICAR in developing and disseminating climate-smart agricultural technologies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government policies in supporting farmers to adapt to climate change impacts.
Suggest policy interventions and technological solutions to enhance food security in the context of increasing climate variability.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmer inspecting crops in a field, symbolizing climate-resilient agriculture.
GS3.trivia: ICAR is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India.

GS3.headline: CERT-In Issues Warning on Evolving Cybersecurity Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) today issued a comprehensive advisory, warning against evolving cybersecurity threats targeting critical infrastructure and government systems.
• The advisory highlighted the rise of sophisticated ransomware attacks, phishing campaigns, and supply chain vulnerabilities, urging organizations to bolster their digital defenses.
• CERT-In emphasized the importance of regular security audits, employee training, and adherence to best practices for data protection and incident response.
• The agency also detailed new guidelines for reporting cyber incidents and fostering greater collaboration between government agencies and private sector cybersecurity firms.
• The warning underscores the increasing importance of cybersecurity in national security and economic stability in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Basics of cyber security
prelimsPoints: CERT-In issued warning on evolving cybersecurity threats.
Highlighted ransomware, phishing, supply chain vulnerabilities.
Emphasized security audits, training, incident response.
New guidelines for reporting cyber incidents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyber attacks on India's critical infrastructure and the implications for national security.
Analyze the measures taken by the Indian government, including the role of CERT-In, to enhance cybersecurity capabilities.
Evaluate the challenges in combating sophisticated cyber crimes and ensuring data protection in a rapidly digitalizing economy.
Suggest a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, private sector, and individuals to build a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract image of digital locks and network lines, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: CERT-In is the national agency for performing various functions in the area of cyber security as per Section 70B of the Information Technology Act, 2000.

GS3.headline: New Species of Amphibian Discovered in Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the biodiversity hotspot of the Western Ghats.
• The newly identified frog species, characterized by its unique vocalizations and distinct morphological features, highlights the rich and unexplored biodiversity of the region.
• This discovery underscores the critical need for continued ecological research and conservation efforts in the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Scientists believe that many more species await discovery in this region, which faces threats from habitat loss, climate change, and human encroachment.
• The finding contributes significantly to the understanding of amphibian diversity and evolution in India.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: New amphibian species discovered in Western Ghats.
Discovery by Zoological Survey of India (ZSI).
Western Ghats is a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO site.
Highlights need for conservation.
GS3.imageDescription: Close-up image of the newly discovered frog species.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Scheme to Boost Sustainability in Textile Industry
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles unveiled a new scheme aimed at promoting sustainability, circularity, and eco-friendly practices within India's textile industry.
• The scheme provides financial incentives for textile units adopting technologies for water conservation, waste reduction, and renewable energy integration.
• It also encourages the use of sustainable raw materials, such as organic cotton and recycled fibers, and promotes responsible manufacturing processes.
• The initiative is designed to enhance the global competitiveness of Indian textiles while addressing environmental concerns and meeting international sustainability standards.
• Capacity building and skill development programs for workers in sustainable textile manufacturing will also be a key component.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Incentives for water conservation, waste reduction, renewable energy.
Promotes organic cotton and recycled fibers.
Aims to enhance global competitiveness and address environmental concerns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the environmental challenges faced by the textile industry and the importance of adopting sustainable practices.
Analyze how government schemes can incentivize green manufacturing and promote a circular economy in the textile sector.
Evaluate the potential impact of sustainable textile practices on India's export market and global image.
Examine the role of technology and innovation in transforming the textile industry towards greater environmental responsibility.
GS3.imageDescription: Fabric samples with 'eco-friendly' tag, symbolizing sustainable textiles.
GS3.trivia: The textile industry is one of the oldest and largest industries in India, contributing significantly to its economy and employment.
